summary: Full‑bodied audience capture of a stellar early‑80s Mother’s Day show with an explosive Scarlet Fire and flawless Stranger→Terrapin transition.
review: The performance is consistently praised, from a tight first set featuring strong versions of Cassidy, Loser and Music Never Stopped to a second set that “takes off” with one of the best Scarlet Fire renditions ever and a seamless Stranger‑to‑Terrapin segue. Reviewers also note Brent’s early‑80s keyboard work and Jerry Garcia’s incendiary guitar throughout, while the recording is described as fuller than the board, delivering an excellent live feel.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers give 5‑star ratings, uniformly praising both musical performance and sound quality; the show is highlighted as exceptional and the source is called the “best” with a full, immersive mix.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as fuller than the board, capturing the live ambience with clear instrument separation; no technical flaws reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and well‑played, setting up a second set that really takes off; transitions—especially Stranger into Terrapin—are highlighted as flawless and the overall pacing keeps the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly called “totally amazing,” delivering loud, rude, ass‑kicking guitar on Scarlet Fire and a unique dissonant jam in Stranger.
band_member_comments.Keys: Early Brent Mydland era noted for flowing 15‑minute jam during Playing In The Band.
